page: The Microsoft Azure E48s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and compute-intensive tasks. The instance achieves a top-tier result in image processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong performance in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 3551.4 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 190.66 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, write bandwidth for large blocks is average at 69.55 GB/sec, and the Passmark memory score falls into the lower 25th percentile, indicating a potential tradeoff in specific memory write latency or synthetic memory subtests.

Given its high memory density and strong CPU performance, the E48s_v6 is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as enterprise databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and large-scale data processing. The inclusion of AMX instructions also makes it viable for CPU-based deep learning and LLM inference, where it demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs focuses the resource allocation entirely on high-density memory and dedicated compute, offering a specialized profile for enterprise applications that rely on external persistent storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure E48s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache, it delivers 384.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per vCPU). The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong memory read bandwidth and software compilation speeds. However, memory write performance and synthetic memory scores represent minor tradeoffs. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX instructions, delivering strong performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and strong memory read bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
384.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and AI workloads.
Strong memory read bandwidth and top-tier image processing performance.
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimized for external Premium Storage.
Best suited for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and data processing.
